failed_rules.message: LoanType must be Closed End Second for 2nd Lien program
Combined Loan-to-Value 80% exceeds maximum 75% for DSCR Investment: FICO Score 720+, up to $350K
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
Loan amount $168,000 below minimum requirement $3,500,001
Occupancy 'Investment Property' not eligible. Available: Primary Residence
Super Jumbo program only available for Primary (Owner Occupied) occupancy - Non-Owner Occupied not eligible
Loan-to-Value 80% exceeds matrix limit 70% for FICO Score 800, Investment Property, $168,000, DSCR 1.25
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
DSCR income documentation is not eligible for Flex Select — use a DSCR program (e.g. DSCR Supreme, Investor DSCR) - FAIL
DSCR income documentation is not eligible for Flex Supreme — use a DSCR program (e.g. DSCR Supreme, Investor DSCR) - FAIL
DSCR Multi (5-8 Units): Only Residential 5-8 Units and Mixed Use 2-8 Units are eligible
Loan-to-Value 80% exceeds maximum 75% for FICO Score 740, Loan up to $1M: 75% Purchase/R&T, 65% Cash-Out
DSCR tier "DSCR > 1.15%" is too high - No Ratio program requires DSCR < 0.75 - FAIL

income: DSCR RATIOS AND RENTAL INCOME REQUIREMENTS

specifications: Must be current and unexpired government-issued photo identification
Must clearly show borrower's full legal name matching loan application
Must include photograph for identity verification
Acceptable forms include driver's license, state ID, passport, or military ID

reasons_needed.reason: Identity verification required for all borrowers per standard lending practices
Confirms borrower identity matches authorization documents and loan application

specifications: Must be fully executed and signed by borrower
Must authorize retrieval of tax transcripts for verification years
Must include borrower's current address and SSN
Must be dated within acceptable timeframe per guidelines

reasons_needed.reason: Required for all income documentation per Full Documentation guidelines
Enables verification of income reported on tax returns

specifications: Must clearly state intended occupancy as investment/non-owner occupied
Must be signed and dated by borrower
Must confirm borrower will not occupy property as primary residence
Must align with loan program selected (DSCR investment loan)

reasons_needed.reason: Investment property occupancy must be documented for DSCR loan program eligibility
Confirms occupancy status matches loan terms and LTV requirements

specifications: Must be included in or attached to the appraisal report
Must show estimated market rent for the subject property
Must identify the property address as 1408 Tremont Ave, Nashville, TN 37206
Must be completed by the appraiser
Must support rental income calculation for the subject property
reasons_needed: DSCR loan requires rental income verification for debt service coverage ratio calculation
No Ratio DSCR program uses gross market rents from Form 1007 for qualifying
Market rent establishes the rental income potential of the investment property
Required for DSCR loan underwriting to determine property cash flow

specifications: Must cover 2 months as required for DSCR loan
Must include all pages of statements
Must show borrower name Emmanuel Nyarko
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show account number or masked account identifier
Must show beginning and ending balances for each month
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines
Must support funds to close calculation for $35,000 estimated closing costs
Must show sufficient liquid funds for closing and any reserve requirements
reasons_needed: Assets must be verified for funds to close on refinance transaction
NQMF guidelines require assets dated within 90 days of note date
Bank statements provide primary evidence of liquid funds availability
Account ownership must be confirmed to borrower Emmanuel Nyarko
Transaction history allows identification of any large deposits requiring sourcing
Supports verification of funds for estimated $35,000 closing costs
guideline_citations: ASSETS section: Assets must be dated within 90 days of note date
Loan files must evidence sufficient funds for closing costs and debt payoff

specifications: Must be tri-merge or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR)
Must provide merged credit information from 3 major national credit repositories (Experian, TransUnion, Equifax)
Must include all borrowers on the loan
Must show credit scores for all borrowers
Must include verification of all credit references provided on loan application
Must certify results of public record searches for each city where borrower has resided in last 2 years
Must be dated within allowed recency window per program requirements
reasons_needed: Credit score is required to validate program eligibility per NQMF Program Matrices
Tri-merge credit report is required for all borrowers with the exception of Foreign National Borrowers without US Credit
Credit report is needed to verify tradeline and credit history requirements
Required to identify any credit events, public records, inquiries, and mortgage payment history

specifications: Must cover 12-month lookback period minimum
Must identify borrower and property/loan account
Must show detailed payment history including any late payments
Must be from acceptable source (servicer statement or credit report)
Must support housing history verification requirements
reasons_needed: Borrower has existing mortgage on investment property that requires payment history verification
Housing history verification is required for all programs per guidelines
Late payment history may affect qualification or pricing
Mortgage payment history must satisfy program requirements for existing mortgage obligations

specifications: Must be completed using FNMA Form 1025 (Small Residential Income Property Appraisal Report for 2-4 Unit Residential Properties) as this is a 2-unit investment property
Must identify subject property address at 3118 West End Ave, Nashville, TN 37203
Must show appraised value to support LTV calculation for $168,000 loan amount
Must include market rent analysis to support DSCR calculation
Must be dated within 365 days of Note date with recertification required if exceeding 120 days
Must include full interior inspection of each unit
Must include street map showing location of subject property and all comparables
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan sketch
Must include original color photographs of front, street, and rear views of subject property
Must include interior photos of kitchen, all bathrooms, main living area in each unit
Must include analysis of all sales of subject property within 3 years prior to appraisal date
Must include completed Sales Comparison Approach section
Must include appraiser certification and statement of limiting conditions
Must include appraiser's errors and omissions insurance policy valid as of appraisal date
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting DSCR refinance transaction
LTV eligibility depends on appraised value for $168,000 loan against estimated $210,000 property value
Market rent analysis is required to support DSCR calculation for investment property
Form 1025 is specifically required for 2-4 unit residential income properties per guidelines
Property identity must be consistent across loan file for compliance
document_requests.compliance_rationale: NQMF guidelines require completed appraisal report on all loan transactions to assess adequacy of property as collateral. Form 1025 is mandated for 2-4 unit residential properties with market rent analysis required for DSCR loans.

specifications: Must be completed for subject property at 3118 West End Ave, Nashville, TN 37203
Must show market rent for both units in the 2-unit investment property
Must be completed by the appraiser as part of the appraisal process
Must align with rental income analysis used for DSCR calculation
Must demonstrate market rent reasonableness through comparable rental data
reasons_needed: DSCR calculation requires market rent support for investment property income
Guidelines require Form 1007 for all 1-4 unit investment properties when rental income is used for qualifying
Market rent validation is essential for debt service coverage ratio analysis
document_requests.compliance_rationale: NQMF guidelines specifically require Single Family Comparable Rent Schedule (FNMA Form 1007) for all 1-unit Investment Properties when rental income is used to qualifying. Extended to 2-4 unit properties for DSCR analysis.

specifications: Must be provided by Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA)
Must identify subject property at 3118 West End Ave, Nashville, TN 37203
Must accurately reflect flood zone designation
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA) zones A or V
Must show if property is in community participating in FEMA program
Must be consistent with flood zone information in appraisal report
reasons_needed: Determination of flood insurance requirement depends on FEMA flood zone designation
Required to establish whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area requiring flood insurance
Compliance with federal flood insurance requirements
document_requests.compliance_rationale: NQMF guidelines require determination whether subject property is in flood zone must be established by Flood Certificate provided by FEMA. Required for all transactions to determine flood insurance requirements.

specifications: Must name all persons holding title to subject property at 3118 West End Ave, Nashville, TN 37203 as named insured
Must provide adequate coverage for 2-unit investment property
Must include policy number, insurance company name, and agent information
Must show effective and expiration dates of coverage
Must include premium amount and deductible information
Must include loss payee clause naming NQM Funding, LLC as mortgagee
Must be maintained throughout duration of loan
Property address must agree with note/security instrument
reasons_needed: Property insurance protects lender's collateral interest in investment property
Required for all mortgage transactions to protect against property loss
Ensures borrower has adequate coverage for 2-unit rental property risks
document_requests.compliance_rationale: NQMF guidelines require property insurance for all transactions. Individual property insurance policy must list all persons holding title as named insured with proper loss payee designation.

specifications: Must provide FNMA SSR Score of 2.5 or less as acceptable review product
Must support appraisal value for the transaction through secondary appraisal product
Must be completed for subject property at 3118 West End Ave, Nashville, TN 37203
Must validate appraisal findings and value conclusion
reasons_needed: Appraisal review process requires secondary appraisal product to support appraisal value
Risk management tool to validate appraised value reasonableness
Standard requirement for appraisal quality control
document_requests.compliance_rationale: NQMF guidelines require appraisal review process with acceptable review products including FNMA SSR Score of 2.5 or less to support appraisal value for all transactions.

specifications: Must be FNMA Form 3170 (1-4 Family Rider - Assignment of Rents)
Must apply to investment property at 3118 West End Ave, Nashville, TN 37203
Must assign rental income rights to lender
Must be properly executed and recorded
Must provide lender with rights to collect rents in case of default
reasons_needed: Required for all investment properties to secure rental income stream
Provides lender protection and rights to rental income in event of borrower default
Standard requirement for investment property financing
document_requests.compliance_rationale: NQMF guidelines specifically require 1-4 Family Rider (Assignment of Rents) FNMA Form 3170 for all Investment Properties to secure rental income rights.

specifications: Must identify borrower Emmanuel Nyarko as owner/vested party
Must identify subject property address: 1621 N 6th St, Nashville, TN 37208
Must include complete legal description of the property
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, exceptions, and title requirements
Must show title insurer and effective date within 60 days
Must confirm clear and marketable title with proper vesting
Must show lien position for existing mortgage to be refinanced
reasons_needed: Underwriter must verify borrower ownership and vesting alignment with loan application
Property address and legal description must match appraisal and loan documents
Existing liens must be identified for proper payoff and lien position calculation
Title exceptions and requirements may affect closing conditions and loan approval
Insurable title is required for mortgage lending and title insurance coverage

specifications: Must show Emmanuel Nyarko as current titleholder/grantee
Must identify the subject property: 1621 N 6th St, Nashville, TN 37208
Must include complete legal description matching title commitment
Must be properly executed with notarization and recording information
Must confirm individual ownership (not entity) as indicated in loan file
reasons_needed: Confirms borrower's legal ownership of the subject property
Vesting must align with borrower identity and loan application structure
Required to verify proper title chain and ownership history
Ensures borrower has authority to encumber the property with mortgage lien

specifications: Must identify existing mortgage servicer and account number
Must show exact payoff amount for existing mortgage on subject property
Must include good-through date (typically 30 days from statement date)
Must identify the subject property: 1621 N 6th St, Nashville, TN 37208
Must include per diem interest calculation if applicable
Must be dated within 30 days of closing
reasons_needed: Required for refinance transaction to pay off existing mortgage lien
Payoff amount is needed to calculate net loan proceeds and funds to close
Ensures proper lien release and clear title transfer at closing
Per diem calculation ensures accurate payoff amount through closing date

document_requests.document_type: Borrower Certification as to Business Purpose
document_requests.description: Business Purpose & Occupancy Affidavit declaring commercial business or investment purpose only
document_requests.reasoning: Required for all DSCR investment property loans as this is classified as a business purpose transaction per NQMF guidelines
specifications: Must identify borrower Emmanuel Nyarko
Must identify subject property at 1508 Berry Street, Nashville, TN 37206
Must declare property is for commercial business or investment purpose only
Must be signed and dated by borrower
Must confirm property will not be occupied by borrower or family members
Must be consistent with investment property occupancy type
reasons_needed: DSCR loans must be classified as business purpose transactions per NQMF guidelines
Investment properties require business purpose declaration to comply with non-TRID processing requirements
Occupancy and business purpose representations must be consistent for underwriting approval
Required compliance documentation for all business purpose loans per guideline section

DSCR loans must be business purpose and processed as non-TRID transactions

STEP_00.summary: STEP_00 successfully parsed the loan file for Emmanuel Nyarko's $168k refinance on an investment property in Tennessee. The loan profile shows an 80% LTV DSCR loan with program eligibility for Investor DSCR and DSCR Supreme. Processed 32 submitted documents including appraisal, multiple borrower authorizations, and URLA forms. No contradictions were detected and documents were properly routed to their respective facets for downstream processing.

STEP_01.summary: Generated cross-cutting document requests focusing on essential identity verification, tax authorization, and occupancy confirmation. Created three mandatory documents: Government-Issued Photo ID for borrower identity verification, IRS Form 4506-C for tax transcript authorization as required by Full Documentation guidelines, and Occupancy Certification to confirm investment property status for DSCR loan eligibility. No overlay conflicts were detected in this loan file.

STEP_02.summary: Generated income document requests for DSCR loan structure. Since this is a No Ratio DSCR refinance of an investment property, the only required income documentation is the Form 1007 Rent Schedule to establish market rent for debt service coverage calculation. No traditional income documents (W-2, paystubs, tax returns) are required for DSCR loans as they qualify based on property cash flow rather than borrower income.

Market rent establishes the rental income potential of the investment property

STEP_03.summary: Generated asset document requests for Emmanuel Nyarko's refinance transaction. Analysis of the scenario showed this is a DSCR refinance with no gift funds, large deposits, or business fund usage indicated. Generated one mandatory document request for Personal Bank Statements covering 2 months to verify sufficient liquid funds for the estimated $35,000 closing costs. The request includes specifications for complete transaction history, borrower name verification, and compliance with NQMF's 90-day asset dating requirement.

STEP_04.summary: Generated credit document requests for Emmanuel Nyarko's refinance transaction. Analysis of the scenario showed a DSCR loan with an existing mortgage on the investment property and credit_events listed as ["none"], indicating no bankruptcy, foreclosure, or other credit events. Applied strict relevance gates and determined only two documents are needed: (1) Tri-Merge Credit Report as required for all borrowers to verify credit scores and program eligibility, and (2) Mortgage Payment History for the existing mortgage on the investment property to satisfy housing history verification requirements. Did not request speculative documents like VOM, VOR, tradeline supplements, or credit event documentation since the borrower has good credit with no adverse events.

STEP_05.summary: Generated property and appraisal document requests for Emmanuel Nyarko's 2-unit investment property DSCR refinance. The analysis identified six mandatory documents: (1) Appraisal Report using Form 1025 for the 2-4 unit property with market rent analysis, (2) Form 1007 Rent Schedule to support DSCR calculations, (3) Flood Determination Certificate from FEMA to establish flood insurance requirements, (4) Property Insurance with proper loss payee designation, (5) SSR/UCDP Findings for appraisal review, and (6) 1-4 Family Rider for rental income assignment rights. All requests include detailed specifications aligned with NQMF guidelines and reflect the investment property nature requiring rental income documentation for debt service coverage analysis.

STEP_06.summary: Generated title and closing document requests for Emmanuel Nyarko's DSCR refinance transaction. Applied strict relevance gate to focus only on underwriting-essential documents. Created three mandatory requests: (1) Title Commitment to verify borrower ownership, identify liens, and confirm insurable title with proper vesting; (2) Vesting Deed to confirm Emmanuel Nyarko's legal ownership and authority to encumber the property; and (3) Payoff Statement for the existing mortgage to be refinanced, including exact payoff amount and per diem calculations. Excluded closing-stage documents like Closing Disclosure and Settlement Statement that are generated at closing rather than reviewed during underwriting. The targeted approach ensures underwriter has essential title and lien information while avoiding duplicative requests with other modules.
